text: Transformer T-005 is operating near peak load. Check the winding temperature and determine if it is within safe limits.
characteristic_form: The answer should report the latest winding-temperature reading with unit, compare it to a safe limit, and summarize the result — all from actual tool output.

final_value.threshold_comparison: agent must report whether the latest winding_temp_top_c reading is within safe limits, based on actual sensor data

acceptance_criteria: agent discovers the available sensors via iot.list_sensors before retrieving readings
agent retrieves winding_temp_top_c readings for T-005 via iot.get_sensor_readings
agent compares the actual reading to a stated safe limit

applied_edits: Added iot.list_sensors as the discovery step before iot.get_sensor_readings — both in expected_tools and in ground_truth.ideal_tool_sequence.

Dropped fabricated decisive_intermediate_values.winding_temp_top_c='80' (this was the fixture max, not a typical reading; T-005 winding_temp_top_c: min=21.2, max=80.0, mean=50.4 in data/processed/sensor_readings.csv).
Pinned sensor_id='winding_temp_top_c' so the rubric can verify the agent picked the right sensor without a deterministic numeric ground truth.
Updated nearest_handcrafted_comparator from iot_01_list_transformer_sensors (SGT-001) to iot_04_load_current_overload_check (SGT-012) per reviewer-selected comparator.

nearest_handcrafted_comparator.similarity_basis: both ask for a point-in-time threshold check on a single transformer sensor.
nearest_handcrafted_comparator.novelty_note: different sensor (winding_temp_top_c vs load_current_a) on the same asset; same single-shot threshold-check shape.
